name: Conveyor Line Tracking Synchronization
description: A robotics engineer must program a pick-and-place robot to synchronize its end-effector with a moving part on a conveyor belt. Using the CoppeliaSim ZMQ Remote API, write a Python script to load the 'messaging/movementViaRemoteApi.ttt' scene and simulate a moving part represented by a dummy object starting at [0.3, -0.4, 0.05]. Run a synchronous simulation for 10.0 seconds where the part moves along the Y-axis at +0.04 m/s and the robot's IK target (usually '/target') tracks it with a +0.15 m Z-axis hover offset. Record the simulation time, part Y-position, robot tip (usually '/tip') Y-position, and the Euclidean distance error in millimeters. Export the tracking data to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/conveyor_tracking.csv and a summary report to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/tracking_report.json. The report must include total_simulation_time_s, part_travel_distance_m, catch_up_time_s (when error first drops below 10.0 mm), and the mean and maximum tracking errors after catch-up. Login: username=ga, password=password123.
